{"key_event":"1891 Civil War and parliamentary-oligarchic turn","reasoning":"Nitrate revenues, public works, railways, and territorial consolidation kept Chile’s state very strong, but the civil war, autonomous communes, and parliamentary dominance reduced centralized executive control from its late-1880s peak. Civil society gained through congressional-party power, elite economic organization, municipal politics, and emerging labor mobilization, though participation remained oligarchic and repression limited popular leverage.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":6.9,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":8.3}
